title: Tamil Coconut Rice

ingredients.name: freshly grated coconut
visual_checks: fresh is best; desiccated coconut moistened with a little water works as a substitute
ingredients.notes: fresh is best; desiccated coconut moistened with a little water works as a substitute

subs.name: 3 tbsp coconut milk powder dissolved in 3 tbsp water
subs.notes: mix into the rice after the tempering; less texture but good coconut flavour

subs.name: peanut
subs.notes: more budget-friendly; roast peanuts in a dry pan before adding to the tempering

subs.name: coconut-oil
subs.notes: intensifies the coconut flavour; use the same quantity

steps.text: Heat gingelly oil in a kadai on medium flame. Fry cashews until golden and remove. Add mustard seeds and let them crackle. Add urad dal and chana dal and fry until they turn pale gold.

Add the dried red chillies and fry for 30 seconds until they darken and become fragrant. Add green chilli, curry leaves and asafoetida and fry for another 30 seconds. The oil should be vividly aromatic at this stage.
Add the grated coconut and stir-fry on medium flame for 3–4 minutes until the coconut turns pale golden and any moisture evaporates. The coconut should smell fragrant and toasted, not raw.
Add the cooled cooked rice to the kadai in batches. Fold gently but thoroughly, coating each grain in the coconut tempering. Season with salt.

Scatter the fried cashews on top and fold through once more. Taste and adjust salt. Serve at room temperature — coconut rice is best eaten warm or at room temperature, not piping hot. Pairs well with pappad, mango pickle and a simple raw salad.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: coconut → 3 tbsp coconut milk powder dissolved in 3 tbsp water (mix into the rice after the tempering; less texture but good coconut flavour); cashew → peanut (more budget-friendly; roast peanuts in a dry pan before adding to the tempering); gingelly-oil → coconut-oil (intensifies the coconut flavour; use the same quantity)
